theme: Place heat sources to reach a temperature snapshot
title: Backward Heat Sourcing: Reaching a Temperature Snapshot
innovation_hook: Diffusion is a low-pass filter, so the target's high-frequency content is fundamentally unreachable -- project onto the reachable subspace and spend a few well-placed, correctly-scaled sources.
strategies: do-nothing baseline: emit the all-zero source field, matching J(0) = ||y||^2 (Ratio 0.1)

naive placement greedy: matching pursuit on the raw target residual with source amplitude set equal to the desired temperature -- undershoots by the diffusion attenuation and is lured by high-frequency spikes into the wrong cells

spectral-regularized sparse pursuit (strong): select on the low-passed residual F(res) so unreachable high-frequency detail cannot mislead placement, use the least-squares amplitude <res,atom>/<atom,atom> to undo the attenuation, and add a source only while its marginal error drop exceeds the per-source charge

joint non-negative least squares refinement over the chosen support (reachable headroom above strong that an RL policy can discover): co-optimize overlapping source amplitudes instead of fixing them sequentially
naive inversion trap (infeasible): backward-diffuse / deconvolve y, which amplifies high frequencies into oscillating negative sources and is rejected
